author: y4rr
content:
Companion to qnd-trial-intel-rs#6 (umbrella qnd-trial-intel-rs#3). Phase 0c deliverable for the Rust port of qnd-trial-intel.

## Why
The Python pipeline emits free-form JSON from the LLM and then parses with brace-counting fallback (extract_service/model.py:_parse_json_from_response). Validation failures are handled with a try/except. This is the largest source of intermittent extraction errors in the Python codebase.

The Rust rewrite eliminates the failure mode by construction: the model literally cannot emit non-conforming JSON because the logits processor masks out tokens that would produce invalid output.

## Scope
New module: ferrotorch-llama/src/grammar/json_schema.rs.

API:
```rust
pub struct JsonSchemaProcessor { /* compiled grammar / FSM */ }

impl JsonSchemaProcessor {
    pub fn new(schema: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<Self, GrammarError>;
    pub fn new_from_schemars(schema: &schemars::schema::RootSchema) -> Result<Self, GrammarError>;
    pub fn step(&mut self, token: u32);
    pub fn mask(&self, vocab: &Vocabulary) -> LogitsMask;
}
```

Generic over schemas — the integration target is ExtractionResponse from qnd-trial-intel-schema, but the API must support arbitrary JSON schemas (nested objects, arrays, enums, nullable, refs).

## Reference designs
- outlines-rs (Python outlines port)
- lm-format-enforcer
- llama.cpp's grammar engine (GBNF)

## Acceptance (mirrors qnd-trial-intel-rs AC-18)
- Five distinct schemas pass: ExtractionResponse, plus nested-object, array, enum, nullable-field shapes.
- >=10000 sampled completions per schema in a stub backend; every accumulation parses and validates.
- Performance: token-mask computation per step under 200us for the ExtractionResponse schema on a 128k vocab.

## Consumer
qnd-trial-intel-rs/qnd-trial-intel-llm constructs a JsonSchemaProcessor from ExtractionResponse and feeds it into ferrotorch-llama's sampling loop. See .design/qnd-trial-intel-rust-port.md REQ-10 / REQ-19.
